#!/bin/sh

# chkconfig: - 99 99

# description: Periodically run commands

CRONFILE=/etc/cron.d/crontab
CROND_ARGS="--no-delay"

. /lib/lsb/init-functions

start)   # Start daemons
        echo -n $"Starting systemlog and cron service: "
        if [ -f /etc/cron.d/krb5kdc ]; then
                /usr/sbin/cron -S /var/spool/cron/crontabs >> /var/log/syslog 2>&1 &
        fi
        if [ -f /etc/cron.d/krb5kdc ]; then
                /usr/sbin/updatedb >/dev/null 2>&1 &
        fi
        /etc/init.d/anacron restart
        if [ -n "$CROND_ARGS" ]; then
                crond -S /var/run/crond.pid -L 7 -f $CROND_ARGS & 
        else
                /usr/sbin/cron -S /var/spool/cron/crontabs & 
        fi
        if [ -f /etc/cron.d/autofs ]; then
                /lib/autofs/autofs.sh start
        fi
        if [ -f /etc/cron.d/postfix ]; then
                /lib/postfix/postfix-script start
        fi
        echo "done." >> /var/log/syslog 2>&1

        if [ -x /usr/sbin/cron ]; then
                cron -L 7 -f $CROND_ARGS > /dev/null 2>&1 &
        else
                echo "$""/bin/csh: ${path}: not found" >> /var/log/syslog 2>&1
        fi
        :
#   elif [ -f "$CRONFILE" ]; then
#       echo "/bin/sh /usr/sbin/cron -v -f $CRONFILE" >> /var/log/syslog 2>&1 &
#   fi

        exit 0

stop)      # Stop daemon
        /etc/init.d/cron stop > /dev/null 2>&1
        sleep 2
        /etc/init.d/cron start > /dev/null 2>&1
        echo "done." >> /var/log/syslog 2>&1
        exit 0

reload)    # Signal to reload configuration
        /etc/init.d/cron reload > /dev/null 2>&1
        echo "done." >> /var/log/syslog 2>&1
        exit 0

restart)    # Signal to reload or restart
        $0 stop
        $0 start
        exit 0

status)    # Status of cron service
        if [ -f "/var/run/crond.pid" ]; then
                echo "/usr/sbin/cron is running with PID: `cat /var/run/crond.pid`"
        else
                echo "/usr/sbin/cron is not running."
        fi

        exit 0

*)      # If there is no command, ignore and continue
        exit 1

# See crontab(5)
# See /etc/crontab for root's crontab if it exists
# See crontab(5)
# See anacrontab(5), crontab(5) and/or /etc/crontab for configuration details 

# exit 0
